title: Operator Command
description: Structured operator command that initiates a content run. Validated fail-closed before any queue creation, artifact write, or seat dispatch (spec §6.1): unknown fields or invalid enum values reject the whole command. An operator command enters by exactly three documented routes — an engine CLI verb, a parsed ops-channel message, or a schema-conformant task record under $CONTENT_HOME/ledger/tasks/.

command_family.description: The RUN_* command family. Determines which fields are required and how the run is routed (spec §6.1 v1 baseline families).

brand.description: Brand id this command runs for (matches a brands/<brand-id>/brand.json id). Account class (operator vs brand) lives on the brand record, not here.

platform.description: Target platform descriptor id (e.g. an entry in the platform-descriptor registry).

format.description: Content format within the platform (e.g. single-post, thread, single-post-image, carousel, short-clip). The valid set is platform-descriptor-defined; this schema enforces presence and type only.

mode.description: Publish posture for this run. Defaults to SAFE when omitted, resolving the SAFE-vs-LIVE contradiction fail-closed (RD-16f). SAFE = artifacts only; LIVE_PREVIEW = real approval cards, no publish; LIVE = full pipeline with draft-only handoff.

routing_class.description: Workflow classification. Routing is by classification, not by command family: TEXT_HEAVY = the written argument is the main artifact; VISUAL_HEAVY = the asset is the main artifact (spec §6.1, §8.1).

slot_ref.description: Reference to the calendar slot this run produces for (a calendar slot_id). Required for RUN_SLOT and RUN_TREND_MANUAL.

campaign_ref.description: Reference to the campaign whose claimed slots this command produces for. Required for RUN_CAMPAIGN.

batch_date.description: The calendar day whose slot set this command produces (the unit morning-kickoff emits). Required for RUN_BATCH. Local calendar date.

trend_report_ref.description: Reference to the manually submitted trend report (trend-report.schema.json) feeding a trend-reserved slot. Required for RUN_TREND_MANUAL.

pillar.description: Content pillar / strategic lane for the run (freeform plain text).

theme.description: Theme or topic for the run (freeform plain text).

content_id.description: Explicit content id. Optional; auto-generated when omitted.

media_source.description: Optional media sourcing hint for visual/video runs: a CONTENT_HOME-relative path, a source hint, or a capture directive.

max.description: Upper bound on the number of slot runs a batch command dispatches (the staggered/bounded kickoff control). Applies to RUN_BATCH and RUN_CAMPAIGN.

notes.description: Optional freeform operator notes carried into the run.
